steps.title: Mark the clause boundary
steps.content: The phrase that have their own hillside geoglyphs restricts which Utah schools are being discussed, so it should not be set off with a comma. After that phrase, the text begins a new complete sentence about an enormous BR geoglyph overlooking Bear River High.

steps.title: Avoid comma errors
steps.content: Choices A and C incorrectly place a comma before the restrictive that clause. They also continue into and an, which fails to create a clean boundary before the next independent clause. Choice D keeps the restrictive clause correct but joins two independent clauses with only a comma, creating a comma splice. A period after geoglyphs fixes the boundary and keeps the modifier restrictive. The answer is B.
